trends.name: Orea V4 brewing temperature debates
trends.buzz: Brewers are testing ultra-high temps (97–100°C) with the Orea V4, claiming sweeter, cleaner cups than the old 92–94°C orthodoxy. The flat-bed geometry seems to handle the heat without bitterness.
trends.angle: "Your kettle, your rules—some V4 fans are cranking temps to 100°C and loving it. Try what excites you; there's no wrong dial."

trends.name: Fellow Opus grinder firmware buzz
trends.buzz: Fellow quietly rolled out firmware updates that tweak motor profiles; users report finer consistency and less retention. Reddit threads show step-by-step update walkthroughs.
trends.angle: "Your Opus might be one USB-C cable away from its best grind yet—firmware love for a grinder you already own feels like free gear."

trends.name: James Hoffmann's Hario Switch V2 deep-dive
trends.buzz: Hoffmann dropped a full recipe and comparison video for the new Hario Switch (ceramic version), calling it "the most forgiving immersion brewer" for beginners.
trends.angle: "If you've been pour-over curious but scared of fussy pours, Hoffmann just gave you permission to start easy—and the Switch makes mistakes nearly impossible."

trends.name: Timemore Sculptor 078S hype wave
trends.buzz: The 078S—Timemore's latest conical grinder—is flooding r/espresso and r/pourover with side-by-sides against Ode Gen 2 and DF64. Early adopters love the stepless dial and sub-$400 price.
trends.angle: "A grinder that does pour-over *and* espresso without the four-figure sticker shock? The 078S is turning heads because great coffee shouldn't require a second mortgage."

trends.name: Onyx Coffee Lab's "Geometry" natural Ethiopia
trends.buzz: Sprudge highlighted Onyx's new single-origin that tastes like "blueberry jam and jasmine"—already sold out twice. Roasters are chasing similar naturals from the same Guji co-op.
trends.angle: "When a bean sells out twice in two weeks, you know it's special—track down Guji naturals while the harvest window is open and taste what the hype is about."

trends.name: Kruve SIFTER Pro launch
trends.buzz: Kruve just released the SIFTER Pro, a larger-capacity version of their grind-particle separator, aimed at home baristas who want to dial in distribution curves without lab equipment.
trends.angle: "Sifting your grinds used to be science-lab territory—now you can geek out on particle size at home, or just ignore it and brew happy. Both are valid."

trends.name: Bloom & bypass pour-over trend
trends.buzz: R/pourover is obsessed with "bloom-and-bypass" recipes: 30g bloom, fast center pour, then dilute the concentrate with hot water. Fans say it's foolproof and highlights origin character.
trends.angle: "Think of it as pour-over meets Americano—bloom big, pour fast, add water. It's low-stress, high-reward, and perfect for mornings when your brain isn't awake yet."

trends.name: Lance Hedrick's Weber Workshops KEY unboxing
trends.buzz: Lance just posted a hands-on with the Weber KEY grinder, calling the new DLC-coated burrs "shockingly low-retention" and praising the stepless micro-adjust for single-dosing perfectionists.
trends.angle: "The KEY is Weber's love letter to single-dosers—if you weigh every bean and hate leftover grinds, Lance says this might be your endgame (until the next one)."

trends.name: Acaia Lunar 2 scale firmware 2.0
trends.buzz: Acaia pushed a major firmware update for the Lunar 2, adding auto-tare on cup detection and a "ghost mode" that hides the display during extraction to reduce obsessive staring.
trends.angle: "'Ghost mode' is Acaia's gentle nudge to stop watching the numbers and start trusting your palate—though we won't judge if you peek anyway."

trends.name: Washed vs. natural processing TikTok explainer trend
trends.buzz: Coffee TikTok is flooded with side-by-side tastings of washed vs. natural beans from the same farm, making processing methods clickable for newcomers. Engagement is through the roof.
trends.angle: "Processing used to be insider jargon—now it's TikTok gold. If you've ever wondered why some beans taste like fruit bombs and others like chocolate, the internet just made it fun."

trends.name: Commandante C60 Nitro Blade Ruby Red limited drop
trends.buzz: Commandante's Ruby Red C60 sold out in under an hour; resale prices on r/coffeeswap are already double MSRP. The hand-grinder hype is real and somehow accelerating in 2026.
trends.angle: "A hand grinder that sells out faster than concert tickets? The C60 proves that slow, manual grinding still has a cult following—and limited colors make it collectible, not just functional."

trends.name: Iced pour-over "flash-chill" summer recipes
trends.buzz: As temps climb, r/pourover and YouTube are sharing flash-chill methods: brew hot directly onto ice in the carafe, doubling dose and halving water. It's faster than cold brew and brighter.
trends.angle: "Hot coffee, meet ice—flash-chilling gives you cold brew's refreshment in four minutes, not overnight. Perfect for summer mornings when patience is in short supply."
